forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,36 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: '9080'
+project: ai-panel
+title: Enable ACP elicitation types in the workspace (schema unstable_elicitation + acp 0.12 bump)
+---
+#elicitation
+
+## Context / Why
+Prerequisite for both Rust agent-wrapper elicitation bridges. The ACP elicitation types (`CreateElicitationRequest`, `CreateElicitationResponse`, `ElicitationAction`) live in the `agent-client-protocol-schema` crate behind its `unstable_elicitation` feature. The user approved enabling the unstable feature; this is the deferred follow-up the board's "ACP 0.10 â†’ 0.11 upgrade" project explicitly left open ("Excludes opting into the `unstable` feature unless a follow-up task chooses to").
+
+## Verified findings (so the implementer doesn't re-derive)
+- The **main** `agent-client-protocol` crate does NOT expose/forward an `unstable_elicitation` feature at any available version (checked 0.11.1, 0.12.0, 0.12.1) â€” its `[features]` forwards 10 other `unstable_*` features but not elicitation. So it must be enabled on the **schema** crate directly.
+- `agent-client-protocol-schema` 0.12.0 defines `unstable_elicitation = []` and gates `CreateElicitationRequest`/`CreateElicitationResponse`/`ElicitationAction` plus `ELICITATION_CREATE_METHOD_NAME = "elicitation/create"` and `ELICITATION_COMPLETE_NOTIFICATION = "elicitation/complete"` behind it (see `src/client.rs`).
+- These method names MATCH the TS SDK (`elicitation/create`, `elicitation/complete`) â€” no cross-SDK reconciliation needed.
+- `Cargo.lock` already resolves `agent-client-protocol-schema = 0.12.0` (main crate still 0.11.1).
+- Workspace `Cargo.toml` opens with "NEVER add features or feature flags to this workspace!" â€” that rule targets gating SAH functionality; enabling a third-party dependency feature **always-on** (no optionality) is consistent with "all functionality all the time". Keep it unconditional.
+
+## What
+- [ ] Bump `agent-client-protocol` to `0.12.1` in the workspace `Cargo.toml` (aligns the main crate with schema 0.12; this is the deferred ACP-upgrade follow-up).
+- [ ] Add `agent-client-protocol-schema = { version = "0.12", features = ["unstable_elicitation"] }` as a direct workspace dependency so Cargo feature-unification turns the elicitation types on through the main crate's `schema` re-export. Confirm `agent_client_protocol::schema::CreateElicitationRequest` resolves (the main crate re-exports the schema crate).
+- [ ] Rebuild the 7 ACP-using crates and fix any 0.11â†’0.12 API drift: `agent-client-protocol-extras`, `claude-agent`, `llama-agent`, `acp-conformance`, `swissarmyhammer-agent`, `swissarmyhammer-tools`, `avp-common`.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] `agent_client_protocol::schema::{CreateElicitationRequest, CreateElicitationResponse, ElicitationAction}` compile and are usable from `claude-agent` and `llama-agent`.
+- [ ] `cargo build --workspace` is green; no other crate regressed by the version bump.
+- [ ] The elicitation feature is unconditional (not behind a SAH-defined feature flag).
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] A compile-level test (e.g. in `agent-client-protocol-extras` or `swissarmyhammer-agent`) that constructs a `CreateElicitationRequest` and matches on `ElicitationAction`, proving the types are reachable.
+- [ ] Run: `cargo nextest run -p agent-client-protocol-extras -p claude-agent -p llama-agent -p swissarmyhammer-agent` â€” all green.
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` where it fits (the compile test first). Treat 0.11â†’0.12 API drift as part of this task.
\ No newline at end of file

Confirm `agent_client_protocol::schema::CreateElicitationRequest` resolves (the main crate re-exports the schema crate).
-- [ ] Rebuild the 7 ACP-using crates and fix any 0.11â†’0.12 API drift: `agent-client-protocol-extras`, `claude-agent`, `llama-agent`, `acp-conformance`, `swissarmyhammer-agent`, `swissarmyhammer-tools`, `avp-common`.
